{{Quote|At approximately 3900 BBY, Queen Tasia sponsored one of the last of her world's colonization missions. The three ships—''Beneficient Tasia'', ''Constant'', and ''Mother Vima''—departed Grizmallt and vanished from sight.|A Galactic Alliance historical document|<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>}}
'''''Constant'''''<ref name="GB" /> was a [[Colony ship/Legends|colony ship]]<ref name="TEA" /> that, along with ''[[Mother Vima]]'', served as a support [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for the colony ship ''[[Beneficent Tasia]]''.<ref name="GB" /> In approximately [[3900 BBY/Legends|3900 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> the three ships departed the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Grizmallt/Legends|Grizmallt]] in an expedition to the dangerous [[southern quadrant]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="GB" /> The ships carried [[Refugee/Legends|refugees]] fleeing a brutal [[Grizmallti civil war/Legends|civil war]]<ref name="Nexus">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref> who sought fortune and glory.<ref name="GB" /> The expedition, led by the [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Kwilaan/Legends|Kwilaan]],<ref name="Secrets">''[[Secrets of Naboo]]''</ref> was sponsored personally by Grizmallt's [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Elsinoré den Tasia]] and was blessed by a [[Unidentified famous Jedi Master|famous Jedi Master]].<ref name="GB" />

After losing contact with ''Beneficent Tasia'', the inhabitants of Grizmallt assumed that the expedition had failed. However,<ref name="GB" /> much later, they learned that<ref name="NEC" /> the damaged ships had landed on the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="GB" /> in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="NEC" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Colonist/Legends|colonists]], including a member of the [[House Korden|Korden family]],<ref name="Intrigue">''[[Galaxy of Intrigue]]''</ref> established themselves on Naboo and developed a thriving [[Naboo (people)/Legends|society]]<ref name="GB" /> after [[Human-Gungan conflicts/Legends|clashing]] with the planet's [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. ''Constant'' was later mentioned in a [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|historical document]] published by the [[Historical Council]] in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Constant'' was first mentioned in the DataBank entry for the [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal Naboo]] in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', a [[2001]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]].<ref name="GB" /> The [[StarWars.com]] episode guide for "[[Crisis on Naboo]]," the eighteenth episode of the [[The Clone Wars: Season Four|fourth season]] of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'',<ref name="Crisis">{{SW|url=explore/the-clone-wars/ep418|text="Crisis on Naboo" - ''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide|nolive=1}}"</ref> which aired on [[February 10]], [[2012]],<ref name="Ep guide">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Episode Guide]]''</ref> stated that a Grizmallt colony ship crashed on Naboo, however, it was not specified which one<ref name="Crisis" /> of the three<ref name="GB" /> ships crashed.<ref name="Crisis" />
